titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 591

p.seg: William, king of the Scots, to justices, sheriffs, and all worthy men of the whole of his land, greeting.

I resolutely instruct that wherever a tenant of the monks of Coldingham may discover fugitives and neyfs of Coldinghamshire or of their other lands, he is legitimately to have them, with all things, and their chattels, and I forbid that anyone unlawfully detain them, on pain of my forefeiture.
As witnesses: Nicholas the Chancellor, Matthew the Archdeacon.
